reviews.review_msg: It's sad to see all those negative reviews. Kino is a great philosopher and she puts together good material for a good class. Proactive students who wish to do well should do well in her class.

She's very nice and her class was very easy if you did the work you would get a good mark. Although this is the class I have learned the least in. Nothing was taught and it was extremely boring. It's an easy A though you have to endure the boring class and come out with no skills. But she's nice and does care.

Phil 270 with Kino is a great course. The weekly readings can seem daunting, but you're only really expected to grasp a few key concepts—clearly outlined in the slides. *Please* just hit the books and ask questions when you're confused. Kino responds to emails quickly and is illuminating during office hours. Doing poorly in this class is a choice!
if you take this class after reading the other reviews, you deserve what's coming to you. don't complain. you did this to yourself.
This class is a disaster. Regret not dropping it earlier. Now stuck with Kino's sleep-inducing lectures
